sessions.lever: arg0 reassigned to (u8*)buf inside the decompress-if; gpu_LoadImage(rect,(s32)arg0)
sibling-pattern two-call form with early return on decompress path; cross-jump merge collapses to one call site
sessions.notes: Sibling at line 280-296 of src/text1b.c uses the same idiom. Cross-jump merge (jump.c find_cross_jump) collapses the two gpu_LoadImage call sites into one because they share the [j ; jr ra] suffix and ABI gives identical FUNCTION_USAGE. With this form, GCC emits addiu $s0,$sp,24 naturally (vs the prior session's addiu+move pair) AND the $a1 sources merge in the call's delay slot. Net: 2-insn improvement vs session 1, retiring rules @24/@25/@28 (s0=sp+24 and $a1/$a2=s0 manufactures) by structural choice — they would no longer fire at any cheat-free index because the candidate emits target's exact pattern.

reviewer.evidence: Session 2 lever is the sibling-pattern two-call form already used (and accepted) by the same file's sibling at line 280-296. Sibling matches in pure C without cheats — this is the project-standard 'decompress-or-direct upload' idiom, not a coercion. 6-test checklist: (1) semantic purpose YES — two distinct upload code paths, one with decompress, one direct; (2) human-programmer test YES — sibling proves a real programmer wrote it this way in this same file; (3) GCC-internals NO — the cross-jump merge is a downstream optimization, the C structure stands on its own program logic; (4) not permuter-derived; (5) not in any forbidden family (no dead stores, no DImode, no pins, no volatile coercion, no inline asm); (6) naming neutral (arg0, buf, rect, dim — original names). cheat-reviewer agent NOT invoked yet — recommend invocation before any commit-to-COMPLETED-C is attempted, even though the lever is structurally identical to the sibling's already-matched form.

remaining_gap.summary: ONE structurally-independent diff remains at distance 2: GCC's combine pass merges the two `arg0 += 4; arg0 += 8;` statements (post-flag-check) into a single `addiu $s0, $s0, 12`. Target keeps them split: `addiu $s0, $s0, 4` (in beqz delay slot) followed by `addiu $s0, $s0, 8` (separate instruction). build_insns 35 vs target 36 (the missing instruction is the +8). Score 2 reflects the 2-position misalignment caused by the missing instruction.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: Rules @24 (insert addiu $s0,$sp,24), @25 (subst $a1 from sp+24 to $s0), and @28 (subst $a2 from sp+24 to $s0) are obviated by the session-2 candidate — it emits target's exact pattern for those positions. Rules @12 (subst $s0+=12 to $s0+=4) and @13 (insert $s0+=8) would still be needed to manufacture the +4/+8 split that combine merged. To reach COMPLETED-C (zero rules) requires defeating the combine fold via pure-C structure, which has not been achieved in 7 attempted variants (session 1's 4 + session 2's 3). The do-while-0 exception is narrowly scoped to LABEL_OUTSIDE_LOOP_P / reorg.c interaction per do-while-zero-exception.md and is NOT sanctioned for defeating combine.

next_hypotheses: Permuter from the session-2 base. Score 2 with build_insns=35 (1 short) is exactly the kind of plateau the permuter excels at — set up permuter/func_800484A0/ with the session-2 candidate, target clean from asm/funcs/func_800484A0.s + tools/decomp-permuter/prelude.inc (drop the gp=64 line). Use --stop-on-zero. Likely 0-cost mutations on declaration order / statement reordering might find a form where combine doesn't merge the two arg0+=N statements.

Diagnose the +12 fold via cc1 -da on the session-2 candidate. Confirm combine.c is the merging pass and check whether any RTL-stage construct (BB boundary, use-between-defs) can be reached from C-source restructure. Particular avenues: (a) inserting a memory access through arg0 between the two defs that doesn't emit instructions itself (combine can see the RTL use even if DCE removes the C code); (b) splitting the dim computation into two halfword loads with the +8 increment between them.

Sibling-deep cross-reference at line 252-296 (the matched sibling). Its body uses `arg0 += 4; arg0 += 8;` too AND matches — investigate WHY the sibling's version doesn't trigger the combine fold but ours does. The sibling has more local variables (p_alt, flags, head, dim, dim2) which might give arg0 a different RTL pseudo lifetime profile.

Try restoring the prior session's `arg0 = (u8*)buf` assignment + the new two-call form combined — i.e., set arg0=buf inside the decompress-if BEFORE the early return, in case the assignment creates an arg0 use that combine sees between the two += statements. This may or may not affect combine since the use is much later, but worth one measurement.

rejected_forms.form: Introduce `s16 *p_buf = buf;` at the top, use p_buf in both branches
rejected_forms.reason: Creates a new live local that outlives arg0; GCC allocates it to $s1, adding sw $s1 to prologue + lw $s1 to epilogue (+2 insns) and the buf-pointer access shape doesn't match target. Worse than HEAD.

rejected_forms.form: Assign `arg0 = (u8*)buf` in BOTH branches (if-true and else)
rejected_forms.reason: Emits the assignment twice (once per branch), build_insns 39 (vs target 36). Net negative.

rejected_forms.form: Reorder to `arg0 += 4; rect[0]=arg1; rect[1]=arg2; arg0 += 8;` (interleave stores)
rejected_forms.reason: No effect — combine still merges the two `addiu` insns; the intervening sh stores don't reference arg0 so combine sees no use-conflict. Reverted to keep the candidate cleaner.
Bytewise identical to the if-form (cc1's jump-threading collapses them at the RTL level). Reverted.

rejected_forms.form: Single-statement interleave: `arg0+=4; rect[0]=arg1; arg0+=8; rect[1]=arg2;` from the session-2 base
rejected_forms.reason: Tested 2026-06-07 from the session-2 floor-2 base. Same score — confirms combine merges regardless of intervening non-arg0-touching stores. Reverted.
Tested 2026-06-07 from session-2 base. cc1 collapses the if-block back to early-return via jump-threading. Same score. Reverted.

rejected_forms.form: Pre-compute dim from arg0+8 offset then collapse +4+8 into single += 12: `dim = *(u32*)(arg0+8); arg0 += 8; arg0 += 4;`
rejected_forms.reason: Tested 2026-06-07. The offset dim load creates 1 extra addressing insn and the arg0+=8;arg0+=4 still combine-merges to +=12. Net regression. Reverted.
